methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Hybron is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $30M to date. The valuation universe for Robotics & Manufacturing comprises 1 public comparable (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($30M) as a proxy for seed-stage valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $75M. The peer-median method could not be reliably applied due to insufficient narrow comparables: while 7 same-sector peers exist in robotics and manufacturing, none met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier needed to constitute a statistically meaningful dataset of three or more comps.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $64M to $86M, centered on a $75M median. This single-method triangulation carries elevated uncertainty. Key sensitivities include the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for Hybron's specific market position and capital efficiency trajectory, as well as the scarcity of narrow-stage peer data in robotics and manufacturing. Additional data points from recent seed-stage or Series A transactions in the sector would refine this estimate materially.
